description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a rectangular, exposed cistern mounted above a rounded bowl. The bowl shows signs of mineral staining and is supported by a solid pedestal base that flares out at the bottom for stability. The cistern features a visible flush lever on the right side, and the overall structure is made of glazed ceramic, giving it a slightly glossy, hard surface. The toilet lacks a visible seat, exposing the rim and interior of the bowl. The design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements or visible accessibility features. The color is predominantly off-white with some discoloration and wear, typical of public or older urban facilities.
description: A utilitarian, off-white ceramic toilet with an exposed cistern and visible flush lever, lacking a seat and showing signs of wear and mineral staining.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open bowl, exposed rim, and the cistern with a flush lever on the right.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, pedestal base, and the protruding cistern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the pedestal base, with no visible plumbing connections.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the bowl's profile and the cistern, with the flush lever not visible from this angle.
